title: wedelia macrodonta
intro: Wedelia macrodonta, also known as the butterfly weed or wedelia, is a tropical plant species native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, characterized by its bright yellow and orange flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids. The health benefits of Wedelia macrodonta include its ability to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, Wedelia macrodonta can be used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to create various remedies for different health conditions.
